title: Kaunia lasiophthalma
intro: Kaunia lasiophthalma is a type of plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea or bean family. The plant is traditionally used in medicine due to its reported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and other chronic conditions. The bioactive constituents of Kaunia lasiophthalma include flavonoids and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Kaunia lasiophthalma is often prepared as a decoction or infused in tea, and may also be used in the form of capsules or tinctures.
taxonomy: Kaunia lasiophthalma is classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the genus Kaunia. The genus Kaunia is itself part of the tribe Senecioneae, which contains many species of dandelion-like plants. The Senecioneae tribe is further classified within the subfamily Cichorioideae, which is a subfamily of the Asteraceae family. Kaunia lasiophthalma's classification reflects its botanical relationships with other dandelion-like plants.
